nativeRange.habitat: Shaded forest streams, riverbanks, and waterfall margins where the plant grows epiphytically on rocks and submerged wood

summary: Anubias Nana Petite is among the smallest Anubias cultivars in the aquarium trade, a slow-growing epiphyte whose leaves typically stay under 2 cm. It is widely grown on rocks and driftwood in nano aquariums and bonsai-style aquascapes.
careNotes: Anubias barteri var. nana 'Petite' is a dwarf cultivar developed by Wabi-Kusa Production / Oriental Aquarium and is sometimes sold under the trade name 'Bonsai'. Care follows the same principles as the standard Anubias barteri var. nana, but the Petite grows even more slowly — leaves are typically under 2 cm and the whole plant remains compact (around 2.5–6 cm tall), making it well suited to the foreground of nano tanks or as an epiphyte on small rocks, twigs, and driftwood. As with all Anubias, the rhizome MUST NOT be buried in substrate — burial causes rot and plant death. Attach the rhizome to hardscape with cyanoacrylate glue, cotton thread, or fishing line until it adheres on its own. Lighting is generally low to low-medium; very strong light combined with the cultivar's slow growth promotes algae on the leaves. CO2 is not required but optional injection accelerates growth. The plant feeds primarily from the water column, so liquid fertilization is more impactful than substrate fertilization. Anubias Nana Petite is widely regarded as one of the more forgiving choices for planted nano aquascapes, tolerating a range of conditions that would stress more demanding species.
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division: cut the rhizome with a clean blade into segments, each retaining at least 3–4 leaves and some healthy roots, and reattach the divisions to hardscape. Lateral growth from rhizome side-buds can also be separated once established.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica catalogues 'Petite' (102C) as a Wabi-Kusa Production / Oriental Aquarium cultivar. Their data: care 'easy', growth slow, light low, CO2 low (optional), tolerated temperature roughly 12–30°C, leaves about 1–2.5 inches. Used for cultivar provenance, growth/light/CO2 classification, and broad temperature envelope.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked native range (West Africa), light (low), CO2 (not necessary, optional benefit), growth (slow–moderate), propagation by rhizome division, and the critical 'do not bury the rhizome' warning. pH range stated as 6.0–7.5.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked slow growth and epiphytic attachment to rock/driftwood. Confirmed hardiness of tissue-cultured Petite. Listed size for standard nana (5–10 cm) used as upper bound on the broader cultivar height profile; Petite itself is smaller.

additional.name: The Aquarium Adviser
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature range (22–27°C optimum), pH (5.5–9.0 tolerated), hardness (50–150 ppm, ≈ 3–8 gH), placement (foreground/epiphyte), and propagation (rhizome division). Confirmed slow growth and rhizome-burial warning.
